description: Your university rocketry team's recent launch of the dual-deploy rocket underperformed its OpenRocket simulation, achieving an apogee of 415 meters on its base H-class motor. Calibrate the aerodynamic drag properties of the rocket design at /home/ga/Documents/rockets/calibration_task.ork to match this 415-meter apogee without adding physical mass, altering component dimensions, or modifying launch conditions. Create a new motor configuration and flight simulation using an Aerotech I-class motor (e.g., I200W, I284W, or I211W) that inherits the calibrated drag settings. Export the time-series flight data for both simulations to /home/ga/Documents/exports/calibration_flight.csv and /home/ga/Documents/exports/prediction_flight.csv. Save the modified rocket to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/calibrated_rocket.ork and write a post-flight analysis report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/calibration_report.txt documenting the calibration method, the matched apogee, and the predicted apogee for the I-class motor.
